title: Dub Screenings Merch
wikitext:
[]: Dub Screenings Merch?             Quote Post by iamsolidsnake » Sat Jul 11, 2009 9:40 pm I did a search and I couldn't find anything, so I was wondering for all the folks who have gone or are going to recent/upcoming screenings, have you seen any related merch? I'm most likely going to the Atlanta screening, and I'd like to know if I should hold out five or so bucks for a program or something of the like. Other such collectibles and what not. Anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 11, 2009 9:43 pm You think anyone in America is going to merchandise Rebuild of Evangelion in theaters? We're lucky they're even putting it in theaters. We'll be even luckier if they're decent enough to have subtitled screenings. Don't hold your breath dude.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jul 12, 2009 1:07 am The Eva Monkey wrote: We'll be even luckier if they're decent enough to have subtitled screenings.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jul 12, 2009 3:10 am Doubt it.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 12, 2009 11:38 am The Eva Monkey wrote: We'll be even luckier if they're decent enough to have subtitled screenings.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 12, 2009 11:59 am BrikHaus wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: We'll be even luckier if they're decent enough to have subtitled screenings.

[]:             Quote Post by iamsolidsnake » Sun Jul 12, 2009 5:36 pm * shakes head * So much for asking an innocent question. To those who have been to any of these screenings, has there been anything? The fact that this getting a release at all makes it prime ground for cheap merchandising at high prices. A translation or the original theater program with the same background graphics could make a lot of side coin for Funi. Its just unassuming enough, and probably cheap enough, to garner a few purchases. Like I said, I'd buy one.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 12, 2009 8:14 pm iamsolidsnake wrote: The fact that this getting a release at all makes it prime ground for cheap merchandising at high prices.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Sun Jul 12, 2009 9:04 pm The problem is that lot of times when US studios get the rights to an anime property, they don't get any merchandising rights. They are very limited in what they can produce or how its distributed. They can market a film/show itself but they often can't produce any actual merchandise that they can sell for profit that is not the film/show itself.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 12, 2009 11:30 pm BattleMonkey wrote: The problem is that lot of times when US studios get the rights to an anime property, they don't get any merchandising rights.
